name: USS PC-603 (PC-603)
events: 7 Jan 1944 Around 1330 VW/7, USS Skate (T/Cdr. E.B. McKinney, USN) ended her second war patrol at Pearl Harbor. She was escorted in by USS PC 603 (Lt. E.D. Gasson, USNR) which had joined around 0810 VW/7.

USS Skate was then taken in hand for post patrol repairs / refit by USS Bushnell (T/Capt. C.T. Bonney, USN). It was noted that the material condition of USS Skate was worse then what was to be expected after two war patrols.
7 Feb 1944 USS Swordfish (Capt. K.G. Hensel, USN) ended her 10th war patrol at Pearl Harbor. She was escorted in by USS PC 603 (Lt. E.D. Gasson, USNR).

15 Aug 1944 At 1330 VW/15, USS Searaven (T/Cdr. M.H. Dry, USN) departed the Submarine Base at Pearl Harbor for her 12th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off the Kuril Islands via Midway where she was to top off with fuel and water before proceeding to her patrol area. She was escorted out by USS PC-603 (Lt.(jg) S. Powel, Jr., USNR) which escorted the submarine until 2015 VW/15. (1)

25 Dec 1944 At 1030 VW/25, USS Searaven (T/Lt.Cdr. R. Berthrong, USN) ended her 13th war patrol at Pearl Harbor. She was escorted in by USS PC-603 (Lt. S. Powel, Jr., USNR) which had joined at 0613 VW/25.
USS Searaven was then taken in hand for a post patrol refit to make her fit for training duties to which she was now assigned. (2)

27 Dec 1944 At 0620 W/27, the submarines USS Dragonet (T/Cdr. J.H. Lewis, USN), which was on passage from Midway to Pearl Harbor, and USS Haddo (T/Lt.Cdr. F.C. Lynch, Jr., USN) which is on passage to Pearl Harbor from a patrol off the Philippines to Pearl Harbor via Saipan, were joined by the patrol vessel USS PC-603 (Lt. S. Powel, Jr., USNR) which was to escort them into Pearl Harbor.

But before they could do so they were to be joined by the submarine USS Gar (T/Lt.Cdr. M. Ferrara, USN) which is on passage to Pearl Harbor from a patrol off the Philippines to Pearl Harbor via Saipan. She joined at 0812 W/27 after which the submarines and their escort proceeded to Pearl Harbor arriving around 1115 W/27.
